content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed the iterative_minimal workflow implementation end-to-end. Core loop logic, stop conditions (stability vs max rounds), git-based diff detection with filesystem fallback, state persistence/resume, and flow semantics are all correct. Config handling adds nested iterative_minimal settings cleanly with proper defaults (max=10, stable=2) and .max(1) guards. Journal events are well-structured and integrated with run tail/status UX. Amendment re-entry correctly resets iteration state via carry_forward_iterative_state (cursor change clears state). Existing flows (minimal, standard, docs_change, ci_improvement) are unaffected — additive change as required. Backward compatibility preserved via #[serde(default)] on the new ActiveRun field. Test coverage is comprehensive: unit tests for stop-condition logic and diff fingerprint (including dirty-file tracking and non-git fallback), integration tests for full iterative_minimal flow with stubbed implementer, and negative test confirming minimal flow emits no iterative events. No critical bugs or significant correctness gaps identified within the current bead's scope.

## Amendments

None.
